content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as recursively self-modifying agents in their own right, where the memory architecture itself actively selects which new experiences to encode, forget, and re-prioritize rather than passively storing inputs selected by the main agent. This creates a nested agent structure where the memory subsystem drives the main agent’s curiosity via intrinsic reward signals tied to under-explored encoded memory patterns.
